title: To Thee, Loved Nith.
content:
I.

        To thee, lov'd Nith, thy gladsome plains,
            Where late wi' careless thought I rang'd,
        Though prest wi' care and sunk in woe,
            To thee I bring a heart unchang'd.

II.

        I love thee, Nith, thy banks and braes,
            Tho' mem'ry there my bosom tear;
        For there he rov'd that brake my heart,
            Yet to that heart, ah! still how dear!
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To Thee, Loved Nith."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
